one_time_description: Transfer to deferred revenue (non-recurring adjustment reducing surplus)
economic_notes: Band receives significant funding through Comprehensive Funding Arrangement with Indigenous Services Canada and First Nations Health Authority. COVID-19 pandemic noted as subsequent event with potential material impact on operations.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2020, not 2024. Gitwangak Band is located in BC (Gitxsan Nation area). One consolidated subsidiary: Gitwangak Education Society. No GBEs. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($4,804,022) + CMHC ($22,379) + FNESC ($271,882) + FNHA ($1,180,136) + New Relationship Trust ($5,000) + First People's Heritage Fund ($165,382) + Province of BC ($23,728) + Tricorp ($73,095) = $6,545,624; OSR = $7,428,437 - $6,545,624 = $882,813. Note: consolidated totals in segmented disclosure show ISC as $4,924,918 with prior year recovery of ($120,896) netting to $4,804,022 on the summary statement. COVID-19 subsequent event disclosed. No remuneration schedule included in the document. Capital Fund expenses of $444,361 included in total expenses. Annual debt service reflects principal repayments only ($24,216); demand loan repayment of $82,109 also made during the year.
